title: AN ACT TO AMEND REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED NINE HUNDRED TWENTY-FOUR, AS AMENDED BY REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED THIRTY-THREE HUNDRED FORTY-FIVE, ENTITLED "AN ACT STANDARDIZING THE SALARIES OF MUNICIÂ­PAL JUDGES IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CLASSIFICATION OF CHARTERED CITIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES."

chunks.text:
H. No. 5434 / 60 OG No. 5, 645 (February 3, 1964)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 3820, June 22, 1963 ]

AN ACT TO AMEND REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED NINE HUNDRED TWENTY-FOUR, AS AMENDED BY REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED THIRTY-THREE HUNDRED FORTY-FIVE, ENTITLED "AN ACT STANDARDIZING THE SALARIES OF MUNICIÂPAL JUDGES IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CLASSIFICATION OF CHARTERED CITIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES."

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SEC. 2. Section two of the same Act, as amended is hereby further amended to read as follows:

"SEC. 2. The municipal judges shall be entitled to thirty days vacation leave with pay for each year of service.

"Any provision of law to the contrary notwithstanding the designation of the positions of municipal judges in chartered cities in the Philippines is hereby changed to that of city judges, and that of the municipal courts to that of city courts."

SEC. 3. The sum of two hundred seven thousand eight hundred twenty pesos or so much thereof as may be necessary is hereby appropriated, out of the funds in the National Treasury not otherwise appropriated, to carry out the provision of this Act for the fiscal year nineÂteen hundred sixty-three to nineteen hundred sixty-four. Thereafter, said amount or so much thereof, shall be included in the annual General Appropriation Acts.

SEC. 4. All Acts or parts of Acts inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed.

SEC. 5. This Act shall take effect on July first, nineteen hundred sixty-three.

Approved, June 22, 1963.
